docs.title: What does this
docs.text: Celery Task Manager was made by 4geeks.com to watch the activity of Celery, and it is used to see which tasks have bugs, this task provides an extra layer to avoid forgetting any data due to some Redis shutdown that should remove all tasks scheduled.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
You should install celery-task-manager with a few optional dependencies running:

pip install celery-task-manager[django,circuitbreaker]

docs.title: django
docs.text: • django

docs.title: circuitbreaker
docs.text: • circuitbreaker

docs.title: Schedule Tasks
docs.text: You should schedule a execution to be executed after a provided time, you need to have set-up task_manager command to use this feature. It is used frequently to make sure that the execution will be executed even if Redis was turned off.

docs.title: Using schedule_task
docs.text: It returns an instance of ScheduledTaskManager.

docs.title: arguments
docs.text:
• task: a Celery task.
  • eta: when it will be executed, it requires a number + a unit, example: 2w is 2 weeks.

docs.title: ETA units
docs.text:
• s: seconds.
  • m: minutes.
  • h: hours.
  • d: days.
  • w: weeks.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
import logging
from task_manager.django.actions import schedule_task
from .tasks import async_remove_from_organization


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@receiver(pre_delete, sender=CohortUser)
def delete_cohort_user(sender, instance, **_):
    # never ending cohorts cannot be in synch with github
    if instance.cohort.never_ends:
        return None

    logger.debug('Cohort user deleted, removing from organization')
    args = (instance.cohort.id, instance.user.id)
    kwargs = {'force': True}

    manager = schedule_task(async_remove_from_organization, '1w')
    if not manager.exists(*args, **kwargs):
        manager.call(*args, **kwargs)

docs.title: ScheduledTaskManager
docs.text: This class manages the scheduling tasks

docs.title: Arguments
docs.text:
• task: a Celery task.
  • eta: when it will be executed, it requires a number + a unit, example: 2w is 2 weeks.

docs.title: call
docs.text: Schedule a task execution. It accepts the arguments whichever you should pass to delay method.

docs.title: call
docs.text: Shortcut method that uses call method internally.

docs.title: acall
docs.text: Asynchronous version of call method.

docs.title: exists
docs.text: Return True if the task exists, False otherwise. It accepts the arguments whichever you should pass to delay method.

docs.title: filter
docs.text: Get a queryset of ScheduledTask's. It accepts the arguments whichever you should pass to delay method.

docs.title: See all scheduled tasks in Django Admin
docs.text: Go to /admin/task_manager/scheduledtask/.

docs.title: Add task_manager to your scheduler
docs.text: python manage.py task_manager manage all related to the task manager.

docs.title: Task
docs.text: task is a regular decorator that wraps a Celery shared_task to use Task Manager. It could track and monitor your tasks executions and protect your task queue against a Redis shutdown process.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: All parameters are the same that get a celery shared_task.

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
import logging

from task_manager.core.exceptions import AbortTask, RetryTask
from task_manager.django.decorators import task

from breathecode.utils.decorators import TaskPriority
from breathecode.services.google_cloud.storage import Storage
from .models import Asset


logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@task(priority=TaskPriority.ACADEMY.value)
def async_delete_asset_images(asset_slug, **_):

    asset = Asset.get_by_slug(asset_slug)
    if asset is None:
        raise RetryTask(f'Asset with slug {asset_slug} not found')

    storage = Storage()
    for img in asset.images.all():
        if img.assets.count() == 1 and img.asset.filter(slug=asset_slug).exists():
            extension = pathlib.Path(img.name).suffix
            cloud_file = storage.file(asset_images_bucket(), img.hash + extension)
            cloud_file.delete()
            img.delete()
        else:
            img.assets.remove(asset)

        logger.info(f'Image {img.name} was deleted')

docs.title: Available exceptions
docs.text: Task manager listens for our exceptions, and it modifies the behavior of the execution, all exceptions that are not included in this list are marked as an ERROR.

docs.title: AbortTask
docs.text: Abort the execution of this task, because the requirements were not met.

docs.title: RetryTask
docs.text: Mark this task to be retried, due to the database does not include the required content yet.

docs.title: CircuitBreakerError
docs.text: Mark this task to be retried. due to an error that occurred in another service.

docs.title: See all executions in Django Admin
docs.text: Go to /admin/task_manager/taskmanager/ you should see, manage and find all data collected through of Task Manager.
